content: First, there is no quick solution, no 'white list' in Thunderbird. This URL has info on available options: to aid you: https://blog.thunderbird.net/2024/09/thunderbird-and-spam/ additionally, you might check your email provider's options, as many have white lists and spam filters that work at the server level prior to the messages being available to Thunderbird. There are also some third-party products, such as Mailwasher, that can do wonders (that's what I use). You can also create a filter for known senders whose messages go to spam. Good luck.

content:
Please quote the message that's asking for the password, complete and verbatim, or post a screen shot.

The only time Thunderbird itself would ask for a password is if you have locked access to your Saved Passwords. That feature can be found at <Settings | Privacy & Security | Passwords | Use a Primary Password>.

If it's not that, it can only be your email provider asking for your password. You might find it in the Saved Passwords area I described above. If it's not there, and don't have it recorded somewhere, you will probably have to reset your password on your provider's web mail page.

content:
Please post a screen shot showing an example. You can redact any personal information. I want to see what you mean by "at the bottom of any saved emails". I read your description as saying that this spurious text appears in the body of the message, but I have trouble imagining that.

Does this happen in all folders or only some, or just one?

If the thing happened in just one folder, and appeared in the Message List and/or header, I would attribute it to a corrupt folder index. But that doesn't fit your description. If it was that, the fix would be to right-click on the folder name, choose Properties, then Repair Folder.
creator: rednoise

content:
re :everything checks, but when it asks for an address, which is from Xfinity, and a password, which I’m not sure they’re asking for so I tried the Xfinity, Yahoo, and Mozilla passwords.

So in Thundrebird you 'New Account > Email then you entered your name, email address and password (so I'm presuming you know that password) Then you get a browser prompt from yahoo asking for same email address and password. That sounds like the set up has got to the stage where it's creating the Oauth token and needs you to confirm you really are you and to select to allow Thunderbird access to the server. The email address and password is specifically for the account you are setting up. You have already entered that at the start of creating the account - it's just yahoo double checking and asking you to allow permission for Thunderbird to access the server.

IF you had a 'comcast' email address, then it's the password you use to access that comcast webmail account/email address regardless of what server it's on. You will only have the one password to access the webmail account for that email address. But you should have already entered that data when you started to create the account in Thunderbird.

Have you already been migrated over to the yahoo server ? if yes, have you already logged on to the webmail account to view your emails via https://login.yahoo.com/

As per info here: https://help.yahoo.com/kb/SLN36803.html

If no then do it now because that is the password you would use when creating the account in thunderbird.

It's got nothing to do with the Mozilla account password. That's just used when accessing eg: this support forum.

content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content:
Good morning,

this usually happens due to an IMAP synchronization issue between Thunderbird and your mail server. The folders are normally not actually deleted but incorrectly shown under trash after a sync error or folder index problem. check your account via webmail first to see if the folders are still there, as in many cases they can simply be moved back from trash on the server side You can also try re-subscribing to folders in Thunderbird and restarting the client to force a fresh sync. If the issue is still present, removing and re-adding the account in Thunderbird often restores the correct folder structure
